cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands at exactly 3:36. Hmm, let me think. I remember that clock angle problems usually involve calculating the positions of each hand based on the time. Let me start by recalling some formulas or methods for this. First, the minute hand. At 36 minutes, the minute hand moves around the clock. Since there are 60 minutes in an hour, each minute corresponds to 360 degrees divided by 60, which is 6 degrees per minute. So, 36 minutes times 6 degrees per minute
